basic_details.job_title: NHSRCL Recruitment 2026 – 15 Posts in IT, Safety, Data Analytics & Cloud | Vacancy Notice 08–20/2026
basic_details.short_information: National High Speed Rail Corporation Limited (NHSRCL) has released Vacancy Notices 08/2026 to 20/2026 inviting applications from Regular/Contractual Officers and Managers working in CPSEs, SPVs, Metro Railways, Ministry of Railways, and Government Departments for 15 posts across IT, Safety, Data Analytics, Cloud Infrastructure, and AFC Systems domains on an Absorption Basis. Posts range from Assistant Manager (E-2) to AGM/JGM (E-6/E-7). Eligibility criteria are reckoned as on 31 May 2026. Candidates can apply for any one post only from Vacancy Notice No. 08/2026 to 20/2026.

application_fee.general_fee: Rs. 400/- (non-refundable, exclusive of transaction processing charges)
application_fee.details: NHSRCL has prescribed a non-refundable application fee for UR, EWS, and OBC candidates. SC, ST, and Women candidates are fully exempted from the fee. The fee must be paid before submitting the application, and candidates are advised to verify their eligibility and the closing date before paying since the fee will not be refunded under any circumstances. | Transaction processing charges over and above Rs. 400/- are also non-refundable. Candidates must note that only one application can be submitted across all vacancy notices 08/2026 to 20/2026 — applying for more than one post is not permitted.

age_limit.details: Posts Maximum Age AGM/JGM (Safety Operations & Maintenance) Vacancy 14 & 15 52 years All IT/Technology posts (Vacancy 0813) and all AM Safety posts (Vacancy 1620) 45 years

post_wise_qualification.desirable: ME/M.Tech in IT, Computer Science, Data Science, AI, Cyber Security, or Software Systems from AICTE/UGC approved institute

qualification.details: Eligibility is reckoned as on 31 May 2026. NHSRCL has defined separate criteria for employees working in IDA pay scales (CPSEs/SPVs/Metro Railways) and CDA pay scales (Ministry of Railways/other Government Departments). Experience from Teaching, Faculty, Apprenticeship, Internship, Proprietorship, Partnership, or Freelancing will not be counted towards the required years of experience. | Candidates wondering about MACP grade pay should note that higher grade pays granted under MACP by departments will not be taken into account for eligibility. Only the substantive grade will be considered for deciding the grade of absorption. Employees in IDA pay scales who do not meet the IDA-specific eligibility criteria may still be considered if they meet the CDA criteria applicable during their employment.

salary_details.details: NHSRCL offers IDA-pattern pay scales for all posts. The approximate annual CTC figures given in the notification are as follows. Selected candidates will be on probation for one year from the date of joining on absorption. | The CTC figures are approximate. Medical standards applicable to all these posts are Executive/Technical: distant vision 6/96/9 or 6/66/12 with or without glasses, near vision JIJII, and normal colour, binocular, field, and night vision. Lasik surgery and radial keratotomy are not permitted.

selection_process: NHSRCL will use a multi-stage selection process for all posts under Vacancy Notices 08/2026 to 20/2026. The Computer Based Test (CBT) for all posts from 08/2026 to 20/2026 may be conducted on the same day and at the same time
which is an important reason why candidates can apply for only one post.
Computer Based Test (CBT) Interview Medical Examination

Management reserves the right to include or dispense with any of these selection stages as deemed fit. Submission of an application does not entitle a candidate to be called for any stage of the selection process. Depending on the volume of applications received

NHSRCL may raise or relax eligibility criteria or prescribe additional written tests. The management's decision regarding selection is final. Outstation candidates called for interview will be paid travelling allowance as per company rules.
how_to_apply: Applications for NHSRCL Vacancy Notices 08/2026 to 20/2026 must be submitted online as per the "Guidelines to Apply" published on the official NHSRCL website. Incomplete applications or applications without supporting documents will be rejected without any correspondence. The following steps outline the application process.
Visit the official NHSRCL website and refer to the "Guidelines to Apply" document for the complete online application procedure.
Register and fill in the application form in the prescribed format only.
Pay the application fee of Rs. 400/- if you belong to UR, EWS, or OBC category (SC/ST/Women are exempted). Note that transaction processing charges are additional and also non-refundable.
Upload all supporting documents as required by the application portal. Applications without supporting documents will be summarily rejected.
Submit only one application across all vacancy notices 08/2026 to 20/2026. Multiple submissions are not permitted.
Note your registration number and monitor your registered email (including spam folder) and the official NHSRCL website regularly for updates.

NHSRCL will not be responsible for any postal delay, wrong delivery, or non-delivery of communication at any stage. All official communication will be sent to the candidate's registered email address only.

content_sections.heading: NHSRCL Recruitment 2026 - Quick Summary
content_sections.text: This section gives a fast overview of the NHSRCL Vacancy Notice 2026 covering all 15 posts notified under Vacancy Notices 08/2026 to 20/2026. These are Absorption Basis appointments for officers currently employed in Central Government, PSU, Metro Railway, or Railway Ministry roles. All 15 posts are open exclusively to candidates currently serving in Central PSUs, SPVs, Metro Railways, the Ministry of Railways, or other Government Departments. Private sector and freelance experience is not eligible. Candidates may apply for only one post across all vacancy notices 08/2026 to 20/2026.

rows.information: National High Speed Rail Corporation Limited (NHSRCL)
IT, AFC Systems, Data Analytics, Cloud Infrastructure, Safety (Operations & Maintenance)
Online (as per "Guidelines to Apply" on official website)

content_sections.heading: NHSRCL Recruitment 2026 Vacancy Details Post-wise Breakdown
content_sections.text: NHSRCL has notified posts across two broad clusters: IT/Technology posts under Vacancy Notices 0813/2026, and Safety posts under Vacancy Notices 1420/2026. The complete post-wise vacancy breakdown is given below. The total across both notices is 15 posts . All Safety posts are deployable at any location or office of NHSRCL/MAHSR. The place of posting for AGM/JGM Safety posts is also any NHSRCL/MAHSR location. Download NHSRCL Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

tables.caption: IT & Technology Posts Vacancy Notice 08/2026 to 13/2026

content_sections.heading: NHSRCL Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Eligibility is reckoned as on 31 May 2026 . NHSRCL has defined separate criteria for employees working in IDA pay scales (CPSEs/SPVs/Metro Railways) and CDA pay scales (Ministry of Railways/other Government Departments). Experience from Teaching, Faculty, Apprenticeship, Internship, Proprietorship, Partnership, or Freelancing will not be counted towards the required years of experience. Candidates wondering about MACP grade pay should note that higher grade pays granted under MACP by departments will not be taken into account for eligibility. Only the substantive grade will be considered for deciding the grade of absorption. Employees in IDA pay scales who do not meet the IDA-specific eligibility criteria may still be considered if they meet the CDA criteria applicable during their employment.

rows.experience_requirement_ida: Minimum 17 years post-qualification experience (of which minimum 12 years in executive pay scales); working in analogous grade E-7, OR in E-6 (Rs. 90,0002,40,000) for at least 2 years; preferably 10 years in relevant work areas

Minimum 14 years post-qualification experience (of which minimum 10 years in executive pay scales); working in analogous grade E-6, OR in E-5 (Rs. 80,0002,20,000) for at least 2 years; preferably 5 years in relevant work areas

rows.post_s: Assistant Manager (IT), AM Safety posts E-2
rows.experience_requirement_ida: Working in analogous grade E-2 (Rs. 50,0001,60,000), OR minimum 4 years post-qualification experience with at least 2 years in pay scales with minimum bottom pay of Rs. 46,000, OR minimum 4 years post-qualification experience with at least 2 years in E-1 (Rs. 40,0001,40,000); preferably 4 years in relevant field

content_sections.heading: NHSRCL Recruitment 2026 Application Fee
content_sections.text: NHSRCL has prescribed a non-refundable application fee for UR, EWS, and OBC candidates. SC, ST, and Women candidates are fully exempted from the fee. The fee must be paid before submitting the application, and candidates are advised to verify their eligibility and the closing date before paying since the fee will not be refunded under any circumstances. Transaction processing charges over and above Rs. 400/- are also non-refundable. Candidates must note that only one application can be submitted across all vacancy notices 08/2026 to 20/2026 — applying for more than one post is not permitted.

rows.application_fee: Rs. 400/- (non-refundable, exclusive of transaction processing charges)

content_sections.heading: Salary and CTC
content_sections.text: NHSRCL offers IDA-pattern pay scales for all posts. The approximate annual CTC figures given in the notification are as follows. Selected candidates will be on probation for one year from the date of joining on absorption. The CTC figures are approximate. Medical standards applicable to all these posts are Executive/Technical : distant vision 6/96/9 or 6/66/12 with or without glasses, near vision JIJII, and normal colour, binocular, field, and night vision. Lasik surgery and radial keratotomy are not permitted.

content_sections.heading: Surety Bond Requirements
content_sections.text: All selected candidates must execute a surety bond at the time of joining. A separate bond applies if the candidate undergoes formal training. These bond conditions are binding and candidates should factor them into their decision before applying. If training is imparted after joining, the candidate must execute a bond for the cost of training plus applicable service tax, committing to serve NHSRCL for a minimum of 5 years after training completion (excluding LWP/EOL periods), with a 3-month prior notice required before resignation.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: NHSRCL will use a multi-stage selection process for all posts under Vacancy Notices 08/2026 to 20/2026. The Computer Based Test (CBT) for all posts from 08/2026 to 20/2026 may be conducted on the same day and at the same time, which is an important reason why candidates can apply for only one post. Computer Based Test (CBT) Interview Medical Examination Management reserves the right to include or dispense with any of these selection stages as deemed fit. Submission of an application does not entitle a candidate to be called for any stage of the selection process. Depending on the volume of applications received, NHSRCL may raise or relax eligibility criteria or prescribe additional written tests. The management's decision regarding selection is final. Outstation candidates called for interview will be paid travelling allowance as per company rules.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply
content_sections.text: Applications for NHSRCL Vacancy Notices 08/2026 to 20/2026 must be submitted online as per the "Guidelines to Apply" published on the official NHSRCL website. Incomplete applications or applications without supporting documents will be rejected without any correspondence. The following steps outline the application process. Visit the official NHSRCL website and refer to the "Guidelines to Apply" document for the complete online application procedure. Register and fill in the application form in the prescribed format only. Pay the application fee of Rs. 400/- if you belong to UR, EWS, or OBC category (SC/ST/Women are exempted). Note that transaction processing charges are additional and also non-refundable. Upload all supporting documents as required by the application portal. Applications without supporting documents will be summarily rejected. Submit only one application across all vacancy notices 08/2026 to 20/2026. Multiple submissions are not permitted. Note your registration number and monitor your registered email (including spam folder) and the official NHSRCL website regularly for updates. NHSRCL will not be responsible for any postal delay, wrong delivery, or non-delivery of communication at any stage. All official communication will be sent to the candidate's registered email address only.

content_sections.heading: Troubleshooting and Common Mistakes to Avoid
content_sections.text: Online application portals for government recruitment can be error-prone, especially as deadlines approach. The following general precautions will help you avoid common technical and procedural mistakes. Apply well before the deadline: Server load increases significantly on the last day. Attempting to pay or submit on the final date risks timeouts, failed transactions, and missed deadlines. Verify payment before closing the window: If a payment gateway transaction fails or remains pending, wait a few minutes and check your bank statement before retrying. Duplicate payments are generally not refunded. Save your registration number immediately: Note down your registration number and application ID as soon as you register. Without it, recovering your application or accessing admit cards later becomes difficult. Check photo and document upload specifications: Government portals typically specify file size limits (e.g., 20100 KB for photos) and accepted formats (JPG/PDF). Uploading files outside these limits causes silent rejection of uploads. Use a compatible browser: If the portal does not load correctly, try a different browser or disable browser extensions. Avoid mobile browsers for the final submission step. Check spam/junk mail: NHSRCL explicitly advises candidates to monitor their spam folders. Shortlisting communications and CBT admit card links are sent to the registered email only. Do not count ineligible experience: Experience from teaching, faculty roles, apprenticeships, internships, proprietorship, partnership, or freelancing will not be counted. Ensure only qualifying service is reflected in your application. One application only: Submitting applications for more than one post under Vacancy Notices 08/2026 to 20/2026 is explicitly not permitted. Ensure you apply for the single most appropriate post before submitting.

faq.question: Q1. How many posts has NHSRCL notified under Vacancy Notices 08/2026 to 20/2026?
faq.answer: NHSRCL has notified a total of 15 posts across IT, AFC Systems, Data Analytics, Cloud Infrastructure, and Safety (Operations and Maintenance) domains under Vacancy Notices 08/2026 to 20/2026.

faq.question: Q2. Who is eligible to apply for NHSRCL Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Only Regular or Contractual Officers and Managers currently working in Central Public Sector Undertakings (CPSEs), SPVs, Metro Railways, Ministry of Railways, or other Government Departments are eligible. Eligibility is reckoned as on 31 May 2026.

faq.question: Q3. What is the application fee for NHSRCL Vacancy Notice 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates belonging to UR, EWS, and OBC categories must pay a non-refundable fee of Rs. 400/- (exclusive of transaction processing charges). SC, ST, and Women candidates are exempted from the fee.

faq.question: Q4. Can I apply for more than one post under NHSRCL Vacancy Notices 08/2026 to 20/2026?
faq.answer: No. Candidates can apply for only one post across all Vacancy Notices 08/2026 to 20/2026. Only one application can be submitted against these vacancy notices. The CBT for all these posts may be conducted on the same day and time.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary for NHSRCL DGM (IT) and DGM (AFC Systems)?
faq.answer: Both DGM posts are in E-5 grade with a pay scale of Rs. 80,000–2,20,000 (IDA) and an approximate annual CTC of Rs. 38.00 Lakhs.

faq.question: Q6. What is the age limit for Safety posts in NHSRCL Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: For AGM/JGM (Safety – Operations and Maintenance) posts, the maximum age limit is 52 years. For Assistant Manager Safety posts (Civil & Track, Electrical, Signalling & Telecom, Rolling Stock, and Operations), the maximum age limit is 45 years.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for NHSRCL Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process consists of three stages: Computer Based Test (CBT), followed by an Interview, and finally a Medical Examination. Management may include or dispense with any of these stages as deemed appropriate.

faq.question: Q8. What is the surety bond amount for NHSRCL selected candidates?
faq.answer: The surety bond on joining is Rs. 3,00,000 for E-2 grade, Rs. 4,00,000 for E-4, E-5, and E-6 grades, and Rs. 5,00,000 for E-7 grade. Candidates must serve a minimum of 3 years and give 90 days' notice before resignation.

faq.question: Q9. What experience is NOT counted towards eligibility in NHSRCL Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Experience in Teaching, Faculty roles, Apprenticeship, Internship, Proprietorship, Partnership, and Freelancing will not be considered as part of the required years of post-qualification experience.

faq.question: Q10. Will MACP grade pay be considered for NHSRCL absorption eligibility?
faq.answer: No. For employees in CDA pay scales, higher grade pays granted under MACP will not be taken into account. Only the substantive grade will be considered for deciding the grade of absorption into NHSRCL.

faq.answer: NHSRCL has notified a total of 15 posts across IT, AFC Systems, Data Analytics, Cloud Infrastructure, and Safety (Operations and Maintenance) domains under Vacancy Notices 08/2026 to 20/2026.
